titleCard.elements: Black #0c0c0e full frame from the 60.57 cut. (1) 60.68 — wordmark 'Omnibus' Helvetica Neue 700, 128px, INK #f6f6f4, letter-spacing -0.035em, centered, baseline y=500; fade 0->1 over 0.28s with tracking easing from -0.010em to -0.035em (ease-out cubic, no overshoot). (2) 60.96 — hairline rule 2px x 64px, FAINT rgba(246,246,244,0.26), centered at y=560; scales from 0 to 64px width over 0.22s ease-out. (3) 61.06 — tagline Menlo 22px ALL-CAPS MUTED rgba(246,246,244,0.62), letter-spacing 0.14em, centered y=604: 'YOUR LAPTOPS · YOUR IDEAS · YOUR MODELS'; fade 0.25s. (4) 61.30 — command line Menlo 20px centered y=656: '$' in FAINT, ' npx omnibus' in INK, followed by an 11x22px block cursor in MUTED that blinks once (on 61.55-61.80); fade 0.25s. All elements hold to 61.80, hard end, no fade-out. AUDIO SYNC (locked): the 60.68 accent = the pitched-down divehit SFX end-hit (mix cue moved to 60.68) landing on the same frame as the wordmark fade-in start, riding on the music swell to -10. It is NOT a cadential music downbeat — per the music plan the track sits on a sustained unresolved pad from ~60.5, no cadence, alive at ~-13 at the 61.8 hard end (colon, not period).

moves.reason: Dust-blow reveal: slow lean-in as the dust comes off the dusty HP (event 3.0-5.5). Ends 5.90, settled before the 6.37 cut to presenter. Curiosity beat with no VO; pairs with dust-blow SFX. Verified cut-safe (1.50 < move < 6.37).

moves.anchor: presenter face (HP laptop kept in frame at bottom)
moves.reason: Hard step exactly on word start of 'slow' (words.json 11.16) - the reason the laptop was abandoned. Held flat at 1.06 through 'too guilty to throw it away'; hold ends where move 3 takes over. Within the 6.37-18.87 shot, cut-safe.

moves.anchor: presenter face (HP laptop kept in frame at bottom)
moves.reason: Continues from the held punch: slow squeeze starting on word start 'statistically' (17.04) as the coworkers' verdict lands. Same anchor as move 2 so there is no lateral drift. Ends 18.83, one frame before the 18.87 cut, which resets scale to 1.00 for free (no release move needed).

moves.anchor: glowing internals of x-ray hologram (RAM/CPU cluster)
moves.reason: Ken Burns into the x-ray: starts on word start 'laptop' (21.84), right as the lid turns translucent (~21.5), and creeps through '16 gigabytes... eight cores... laying around idly', ending at end of 'idly' (29.34). Hologram glow is not camera motion, so nothing is fought. Pairs with hologram hum.

moves.anchor: glowing internals of x-ray hologram (RAM/CPU cluster)
moves.reason: Stat punch 1 on word start 'nine' (30.10) - 'nine of these in the average house'. Steps up from the Ken Burns resting scale; hold ends where stat punch 2 fires. Same anchor, so it reads as one escalating system.

moves.anchor: glowing internals of x-ray hologram (RAM/CPU cluster)
moves.reason: Stat punch 2 on word start '62' (31.46) - the biggest number gets the maximum scale (1.12 cap). NOW UNIFIED: the 62,000,000 TONNES stat card enters at 31.46 too, and the music downbeat nudge targets 31.46 - punch, card, and beat are one accent. Held to 33.94, released by the 33.97 cut. Cut-safe: never crosses the cut.

Word start 'billion' (38.56): frame steps toward the mistral-7b.gguf Properties window just as the split-flap digits begin locking 7,000,000,000 (38.80-39.40). Safely clear of the no-move dive window (37.30-37.95). Held through the digit lock; split-flap flutter SFX under it.

Quick 0.3s release landing on word start 'at' (40.06), fully home at 40.36 - four frames before Notepad opens at 40.40. From here to 49.57 the Windows world's own choreography (toasts, BLOCK dialog, snap 44.83-46.10, minimizes 47.7-48.5, shutdown) plays at 1.00, never fought.

The one permitted shake: catch impact at 51.95 (cut to hallway 51.73, so cut-safe). 0.35s crop jitter, amplitude ~8px at 1080p decaying to 0, from 1.05 overscan settling to 1.00 by 52.30. FRAME LOCK: shake start, catch-foley transient, and the music bar-1 stab all carry the identical timestamp 51.95 and must render on the same output frame - do not let the three departments round differently.

Final line: push begins on word start 'Watch' (whisper 'What', 58.04) and completes exactly on the last word 'job' end (60.12). Holds still through the breath before the 60.57 cut to black / title card. The closing invitation gets the film's last forward motion.

cues.note: Cold open: drawer slide+creak. Trim the 4.0s clip to its first ~1.45s: slide onset at 0.05, wooden stop/creak peak ~1.30, fully out before the 1.50 scene cut. Featured — no VO yet, music is barely in.
Dust blown off the HP. Use the strongest ~1.3s puff from the 3.0s clip, plume onset synced ~3.05. Music-focus beat (no VO until 6.40) — SFX sits inside the swell, not on top of it.

X-ray IGNITION: first ~1.6s of the clip (power-up sweep) as the lid turns translucent ~21.5; 300ms equal-power crossfade into the sustain cue. NOTE: ignition+sustain are ONE continuous hologram voice on the bus (counts as 1 layer for stacking). DECISION: no whoosh at the 18.87 cut — it lands mid-word 'statistically' (17.04-19.04); the music pulse carries that cut.

Hologram SUSTAIN under the stats VO: loop the steady-state 3.0-7.0s region (250ms equal-power loop crossfades) to reach 31.5; fade 30.9-31.8 as the hologram dissolves. Hard rule: never masks '16 gigabytes / eight cores / nine of these'.
RAM module glow, aligned to '16' spoken 22.76. Garnish only — cut if it clutters.
CPU glow, aligned to 'eight cores' (24.50). Garnish only.

Lid OPENING: reverse the lidclose clip so the latch click reads as an unlatch at 34.30 with a faint hinge tail to ~35.0. This practical foley carries the 33.97 cut — no added transition whoosh; the dive riser owns the next airspace.

THE DIVE. Keep only the tail ~2.5s of the 4.0s clip and slide it so the impact transient lands exactly 37.95 (locate the transient in-file first — do not trust the nominal length). The 2.0s fade-in emerges under 'nobody's telling you' (35.46-36.50), crests through the glass-hole 37.30-37.70, impact 37.95 = loudest SFX moment of the act, frame-locked with the music DROP at 37.95; tail rings to ~38.5 under the Properties pop.
mistral-7b.gguf Properties window opens (event 38.30).
Split-flap digits lock 7,000,000,000 left-to-right 38.80-39.40, exactly under '7 billion parameter' (38.26-39.38). Final tick at 39.40 ~+3dB — the 'lock'. Slightly featured; the ticker IS the number.
Notepad opens (tokens start 40.57).

Token-stream bed: trim the 8.0s typing clip to ~4.4s, LPF at 3kHz so it reads as machine texture. Sits under 'reading speed / locally / privately' — subtle; fully out by 44.9 before the snap thunks (never a 3rd layer over the UI pops).

Wi-Fi disconnected toast — FALLING contour reads as 'off/severed'. Frame-locked to the in-world toast at 41.83; lands on the tail of 'locally' (word runs 41.04-41.88), which is fine at -18 (standard tier, not featured). Do not shift it to chase a VO gap — the picture owns this time.
cloud-sync.exe Block dialog opens (event 42.70).
Pointer clicks BLOCK (event 43.27). Clicks cut through — keep them consistent at -14 across the film.

THE TONIGHT BEAT — 'Omnibus is ready · TONIGHT · 9:47 PM' toast. Slightly featured; onset sits in the true VO gap 43.36-43.90 ('tonight.' ends 43.36, 'The' starts 43.90); decay tucks under the next word onset. The ONLY bright/tonal UI sound in the act — keep it singular.
Snap grid overlay appears (event 44.63) — a breath before the thunks.
Window 1 tiles into the 2x2 grid (tiling begins 44.83; first landing ~44.90).
Window 2 — Fleet window lands (frame-verified 45.37).
Window 3 — Second Brain lands (frame-verified 45.63).
Window 4 lands, grid complete — moved to 46.10 to match the measured end of the tiling range (was 46.0). Four identical thunks = mechanical certainty; do not vary the pitch.
Minimize 1 of 4 (event 47.70). Pitch each successive whoosh ~2 semitones LOWER so the sequence audibly descends toward shutdown.
Minimize 2 of 4 (event 47.97).
Minimize 3 of 4 (event 48.23).
Minimize 4 of 4 (event 48.50) — a hair quieter; the room is emptying.
'Shut down HP-DRAWER?' dialog opens (event 48.70).
Pointer clicks SHUT DOWN (event 49.17).

Power-down: first ~1.3s of the 3.0s clip (descending whine), fully out by 50.3. Desktop dies 49.23-49.57 exactly under the VO word 'off.' (48.60-49.24). CRITICAL: 49.6-50.6 stays NAKED — no room-tone fill, no SFX, music muted. VO alone ('I' starts 49.82) over the silence IS the effect.

Lid closes on its own: trim to the latch-click transient. MOVED 50.9 -> 51.12: the lid seats at the END of the 50.6-51.2 close, so the latch lands at the seat, not mid-swing. Still the first sound after the silence; -14 reads featured without being loud. Tail clears before it would stack a 3rd layer (overlaps only flyoff).

Laptop lifts and flies out the right edge 51.2-51.55: MOVED 51.25 -> 51.20 to start on the measured lift-off frame. Trim the 2.0s clip to ~0.9s of rising doppler, pan center to R60; let the tail bridge the 51.73 hallway cut so the whoosh stitches the two locations.

THE CATCH — impact transient at exactly 51.95, FRAME-LOCKED with the EDL shake start (51.95) and the music bar-1 stab (51.95): all three on the same output frame. Keep ~0.5s of handling/cloth tail through 'settled' at 52.5. Second-biggest hit after the dive.

End hit under the title card: MOVED 60.6 -> 60.68 to land on the same frame as the wordmark fade-in start (title card element 1) and the music swell peak — was 80ms early, splitting the accent. Reuse ONLY the impact transient (no riser), pitched -4 st and LPF 1.2kHz — a darker cousin of the dive hit, bookending the act. Tail fades under the card to 61.8.

musicPlan:
USE OF THE SONILO TRACK (first ~62s of the 3-min generation; sections are nudgeable, so request boundaries first, razor-edit as fallback).

SECTION MAP REQUEST (preferred): ask Sonilo for (a) sparse-build to groove drop at 38.0s, (b) a 1.4s breakdown/mute at 49.3, (c) a bar-1 downbeat at 51.95 — CORRECTED from 52.0: it must land on the catch-impact frame, same frame as the shake and catch foley, (d) a sustained open pad (no cadence) from ~60.5. FALLBACK (razor edit): use track 0:00-0:36.2 of the min-1 sparse build for film 0-36.2, then a beat-aligned 300ms equal-power crossfade jump to the last pre-drop bar so the min-2 groove DROP plays at exactly 37.95 — the drop and the dive impact are the same event. All edits on barlines, 250-300ms equal-power crossfades.

TIMELINE: 0.00 enter at -22 dB rel VO — a pulse, almost subliminal, under the drawer slide. FOCUS MOMENT 1 (3.0-6.4): swell to -14 for the dust-blow reveal — no VO yet, the only breathing room the act gets; ride back down to -20/-22 as VO enters at 6.40 (sidechain takes over). 6.4-18.9: sparse build sits ducked under the confession VO; step up ~2dB at 15.4 ('My coworkers tell me...'). 18.87 cut: no music event — the pulse continuing THROUGH the cut smooths it (why the whoosh was skipped). 21.5-31.5 hologram: keep sparse; the hologram hum at -24 occupies the pad slot, so thin the music here if the generation is busy. A downbeat nudged to 31.46 lets the '62 million' zoom punch AND the tonnes stat-card entrance (both now at 31.46) land on a hit — one unified accent, optional but preferred. 33.97-36.2: build tension; if the track has a pre-drop riser/fill, place it 36.2-37.9 interlocking with the divehit riser. FOCUS MOMENT 2 (37.10-38.0): music and SFX riser crest together; DROP at 37.95 at -12, then settle to -16 groove for the descent 38.0-49.2, sidechained to -20 under dense VO. Do NOT add music accents on the UI beats — the synth cues are the percussion section; the ding at 43.77 must own its gap (the caption plan's earlier suggestion of tick-points at 40.99/41.83/42.61 is DELETED — it contradicted this rule). 49.2-50.7: KILL the music with the power-down — fast 250ms fade starting 49.25, DEAD SILENT until 50.7 ('powered off' only works if everything obeys the shutdown; VO alone continues at 49.82). 50.7: re-enter low (-20) with a filtered/thinned groove (fallback: same groove bar, HPF 300Hz, open the filter over 1.2s). FOCUS MOMENT 3 (51.95): bar-1 downbeat/stab lands EXACTLY on the catch at -14, frame-locked with shake + catch foley (VO is mid-phrase, so a one-beat stab, not a sustained swell), then settle to -18 under the closing lines. 53.9 'This is Omnibus': open up ~2dB, fuller voicing — conviction, not climax. 58.3-60.1 final line: hold steady, no build (the line is the build). 60.57 cut to black: FOCUS MOMENT 4 — swell to -10 under the title card; the accent lives in the SFX end-hit now at 60.68, same frame as the wordmark. The music itself plays NO downbeat there — see end treatment.

END TREATMENT (hand-off, not ending): from 60.5 the music must sit on a SUSTAINED, UNRESOLVED voicing — an open pad/held chord, no cadence, no final downbeat (the 60.68 accent is the SFX end-hit riding the swell, not a music event). Ride from -10 down ~3dB across 61.0-61.8 but DO NOT fade to silence: the cut ends at 61.8 with the music still alive at ~-13, mid-phrase, because the same track continues into minute 2 (the live-demo groove) in part 2 of the film. The title card should feel like a colon, not a period.
levels:
DELIVERABLE TARGET: -16 LUFS integrated, true peak ceiling -1.5 dBTP (safe across YouTube/Devpost embeds/social; survives -14 normalization without repenalty), LRA 6-9 — wide enough that the 49.6-50.6 silence and the 37.95 impact both register, controlled enough for laptop speakers. Master bus: true-peak limiter at -1.5 dBTP, no more than 1-2dB of limiting on the dive and catch hits. Check the whole mix in mono fold-down (UI synths are centered; only the flyoff pans, R60 max, so fold-down is safe).

BUS ARCHITECTURE: VO is the reference bus — clean up first (HPF 80Hz, de-ess, 2:1 compression for 3-5dB GR), normalize so active speech sits ~-18 LUFS short-term; every gainDb in the cue sheet is relative to this anchor. MUSIC bus: nominal -20 dB rel VO under speech (post-sidechain), featured moments -10 to -14 per the music plan (dust-blow, drop, catch stab, title card). SFX bus, three tiers: beds/atmosphere -24 to -27 (hologram sustain, typing, minimize whooshes), standard UI/foley -14 to -20 (pops, clicks, thunks, toasts, lid work), featured hits -8 to -13 (drawer, dustblow, dive impact, catch, power-down, end hit). Light 1.5:1 glue on the SFX bus only; do not compress the dive/catch transients away. STACKING RULE (verified against the locked cue sheet): never more than 2 simultaneous SFX layers — the hologram ignition/sustain crossfade counts as one voice; the typing bed is fully out by 44.9 before the snap thunks; lidclose tail (51.12) overlaps only flyoff (51.2); flyoff tail overlaps only the catch (51.95).

SIDECHAIN (music keyed from VO): threshold set so speech produces 4-6dB gain reduction — cap the duck at 6dB so music never audibly pumps to zero; ratio 3:1; attack 20ms; hold 100ms; release 500ms so the music breathes back in the phrase gaps ('tonight. // The most private...', 'off. // I stopped...'); 10ms lookahead if available. The sidechain handles dense VO automatically; the four FOCUS moments and the 49.25 kill are MANUAL rides on top — do not let the compressor make creative decisions. Optionally key 2dB of SFX-bus duck from the VO for the descent UI cluster (40.4-46.0) where cues are densest. Also duck the MUSIC 2dB under the two featured impacts (37.95, 51.95) only if the drop/stab edits don't already align — if the music plan's boundary nudges land, the accents coincide (both are now frame-locked: drop=37.95, stab=51.95) and no duck is needed.

SOURCE PATHS: footage /Users/chava/Desktop/omnibus/docs/demo-assets/footage/copy_8226FE7C-AE0D-47E4-9685-9194C0BF58D3.mov (strip its live AAC VO to the dialog bus); SFX clips in /Users/chava/Desktop/omnibus/docs/demo-assets/sfx/ (drawer.mp3 4.0s, dustblow.mp3 3.0s, hologram.mp3 8.0s, divehit.mp3 4.0s, powerdn.mp3 3.0s, lidclose.mp3 2.0s, flyoff.mp3 2.0s, catch.mp3 2.0s, typing.mp3 8.0s — durations verified); word timings /Users/chava/Desktop/omnibus/docs/demo-assets/_scout/words.json. HLG note: footage is bt2020nc/arib-std-b67 — comp all graphics AFTER tone-mapping to the delivery transfer so INK #f6f6f4 stays reference white (per caption plan), and per the EDL either keep HLG tags end-to-end or tone-map once globally, never per segment.
changes: SUPERVISOR PASS — every timestamp re-verified against words.json and the measured event table; 8 issues found, all resolved, no ideas dropped. (1) CATCH FRAME SPLIT [check f]: the music section-map requested a bar-1 downbeat at 52.0 while the EDL shake and catch foley sit at 51.95 — a 1.5-frame split on the film's second-biggest hit. Locked shake + catch transient + music stab all to 51.95 on the same output frame; frame-lock language added to all three plans. (2) TITLE ACCENT SPLIT [d]: mix end-hit at 60.6 vs the title card's music hit at 60.68 with the wordmark — two times for one accent. Both locked to 60.68 (wordmark fade-in frame). Also reconciled wording: the music plan forbids a final downbeat (unresolved pad hand-off), so the 60.68 accent is defined everywhere as the SFX end-hit riding the swell, not a cadence. (3) FIRST CAPTION CROSSED THE 6.37 CUT [a,d]: card 1 in at 6.35 put two frames of caption on the drawer-shot tail (the plan's otherwise-consistent 50ms word-lead policy backfires only at this cut). Moved to 6.40 = VO onset; all other 50ms leads verified intentional and kept. (4) STAT-CARD HANDOFF MISSED THE WORD [a,d]: the 62,000,000 TONNES card entered at 31.55, 90ms after '62' (31.46), with the 9-devices card still up to 31.5. Re-timed to out 31.42 / in 31.46 — now one unified accent with the EDL 1.12 punch at 31.46 and the optional music downbeat nudge (music plan updated to 31.46). (5) MUSIC-ACCENT CONTRADICTION [c]: caption notes suggested music tick-points at 40.99/41.83/42.61; the music plan explicitly forbids accents on UI beats. Tick-points deleted — the synth cues are the percussion there. (6) LID LATCH MISTIMED [d]: lidclose cue at 50.9 clicked mid-close; the lid seats at the end of the 50.6-51.2 event, so moved to 51.12. Flyoff nudged 51.25 -> 51.20 (measured lift-off frame). Snap-thunk 4 moved 46.0 -> 46.10 (measured 'grid complete'). Snap-thunk 1 kept at 44.90 (tiling begins 44.83; 44.90 is the first landing and keeps it clear of the typing-bed tail ending 44.9). (7) FACTUAL NOTE FIX [c,d]: the Wi-Fi toast note claimed the pop lands 'in the micro-gap after locally' — 'locally' actually runs 41.04-41.88, so the frame-locked 41.83 toast lands on the word's tail. Time unchanged (picture-locked, and at -18 it is standard tier, not featured, so no dense-VO violation); note corrected so no one 'fixes' it later. The 43.77 ding's claimed gap 43.36-43.90 verified correct against words.json. (8) VERIFIED CLEAN [a,b,c,e]: zero caption time-overlaps across all 40 cards; all Windows-world cards 37.05-49.39 at bottom-180 (clears taskbar y1020-1080 and the white toast x>=1418); no stat cards inside 37.10-49.57; all 10 EDL moves cut-safe against 1.50/6.37/18.87/33.97/49.6/51.73/60.57 with nothing moving during the dive 37.30-37.95, the snap 44.83-46.10, or the minimizes 47.7-48.5; SFX never stacks more than 2 deep at any instant (hologram ignition->sustain crossfade is one voice; typing bed exits at 44.9 before the thunks); quiet-beat captions 49.77-52.45 confirmed minimal per check (e) — plain mono MUTED, zero emph words, bottom-96, VO continues so they must too. CARRY-FORWARD (unchanged and still binding): caption base style + EMPH pop rule with the merged word-start table (E-WASTE=16.40, 7-BILLION-PARAMETER=38.26 echoing the split-flap, OWN=46.00, POWERED=47.80, OFF=48.60, TEAMMATES=52.88, OMNIBUS=54.24 at the 1.15x exception, DRAWER=58.88, LAPTOP=59.24, JOB=59.90, etc.), the triad replacement-card treatment (caption leads, in-world UI answers), 'POWERED OFF.' holding alone over the dying desktop, no captions 0-6.40 and 60.27-60.57, whisper corrections list, StatCard style with cards 2/3 rendered as one persistent card gaining the '8 CORES' line at 24.50, the EDL's 4x-upscale-before-crop implementation note and 1.12 scale cap, and the full mix bus/sidechain architecture.
